mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or t-statistic for return difference versus baseline is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that sustains corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month trend indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns remain positive when INDPRO falls below trend during tightening periods, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio for the signal is < 0.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading below 190.0 signals disinflationary pressure that reduces real discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL falls below 190.0, or signal precision drops below 0.45.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in > 50% of divergence episodes, or correlation coefficient between divergence signal and returns exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0% confirms macroeconomic expansion that supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day returns following GDPC1 releases above 2.0% are negative in > 55% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production above its 6-month moving average signals strengthening supply capacity that alleviates cost pressures, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of instances when INDPRO exceeds its 6-month trend during recovery phases, or signal hit rate falls below 0.50.
